zh.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington -是Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.的女兒，於1145年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾亨廷頓。她於1165年與Sir John de Crawford在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1205年在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德享年60歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington -是Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.的女儿，于1145年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾亨廷頓。她于1165年与Sir John de Crawford在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1205年在苏格兰藍納可爾克勞福德享年60岁，埋葬在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Huntington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

variants.name: Hunt
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a hunter, from Middle English hunte ‘hunter, huntsman’ (Old English hunta). The term was used not only of the hunting on horseback of game such as stags and wild boars, which in the Middle Ages was a pursuit restricted to the ranks of the nobility, but also to much humbler forms of pursuit such as bird catching and poaching for food. The word seems also to have been used as an Old English personal name and to have survived into the Middle Ages as an occasional personal name. Compare Huntington and Huntley .
Irish: adopted for various Irish surnames containing or thought to contain the Gaelic element fiadhach ‘hunt’; for example Ó Fiaich (see Fee ) and Ó Fiachna (see Fenton ).
Possibly an Americanized form of German   Hundt  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - was born in 1145, in Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland as the daughter of Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.. She married Sir John de Crawford in 1165, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1205, in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, at the age of 60, and was buried in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - est née en 1145, à Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, fille d’Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.. Elle a épousé Sir John de Crawford en 1165, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1205, à Crawford, Lanarkshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - wurde 1145 in Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, als Tochter von Earl David de Huntingdon 9th. geboren. Sie hat 1165 in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland Sir John de Crawford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1205 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland und wurde in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - è nata il nel 1145 nel Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland come figlia di Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.. Lei sposato Sir John de Crawford nel 1165, nel Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1205, nel Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington -は1145年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアハンティントンでEarl David de Huntingdon 9th.の娘として生まれた。1165年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードでSir John de Crawfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1205年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアクラウフォードで60歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーペイスリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington -(은)는 1145년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 헌틴그턴에서 Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1165년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 Sir John de Crawford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1205년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 크롤퍼드에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - nasceu em 1145, em Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland filha de Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.. Ela casou-se com Sir John de Crawford em 1165, em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1205, em Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - родилась в 1145 г. в местности хантингтон, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия. Её отца звали Earl David de Huntingdon 9th. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Crawford в 1165 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1205 г. в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret de Huntington - nació en 1145, en Huntington, Haddingtonshire, Scotland como hija de Earl David de Huntingdon 9th.. Se casó con Sir John de Crawford en 1165, en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1205, en Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.
